mission.title: Install and Configure Headlamp on Kubernetes
mission.description: Headlamp is an easy-to-use and extensible Kubernetes web UI that provides a user-friendly interface for managing Kubernetes resources. Installing Headlamp allows you to visualize and interact with your Kubernetes clusters efficiently.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Headlamp Helm repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add headlamp https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/headlamp
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Headlamp
steps.description:
Install Headlamp in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. The official recommendation is to install in the `kube-system` namespace.
```bash
helm install headlamp headlamp/headlamp --namespace kube-system --version 0.40.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Headlamp pods are running correctly in the 'kube-system' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access Headlamp
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Headlamp UI from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/headlamp --namespace kube-system 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall Headlamp from your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall headlamp --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any custom resources associated with Headlamp, if applicable. Check for CRDs and delete them.
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
```

uninstall.title: Verify removal
uninstall.description:
Verify that Headlamp resources have been removed from kube-system.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources of Headlamp.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp -o yaml > headlamp-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Headlamp
upgrade.description:
Upgrade your existing Headlamp installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade headlamp headlamp/headlamp --namespace kube-system --version 0.40.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Headlamp pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the Headlamp pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Headlamp UI not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Headlamp UI, ensure the port-forward command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=headlamp
```

troubleshooting.title: Error logs in Headlamp pods
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors in the Headlamp UI, check the logs of the Headlamp pods for more details.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kube-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Headlamp will result in running pods in the 'kube-system' namespace, and you will be able to access the Headlamp UI through port forwarding. You can manage your Kubernetes resources through the user-friendly interface.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add headlamp https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/headlamp
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
